title: Fix MongoDB Aggregation Pipeline to Ignore Missing Fields

statement.markdown: Write a MongoDB aggregation pipeline that projects a document to ignore missing fields. Given an array of objects, each object represents a document in a MongoDB collection. The pipeline should iterate over the documents, and for each document, it should project the fields that have a value. The resulting projected fields should be returned as an array of documents.
statement.inputFormat: An array of documents, where each document is an object with string keys and values of any type.
statement.outputFormat: An array of projected documents, where each document has only the fields with a value.
statement.notes: The pipeline should handle documents with missing fields, and it should not include fields with a value of null or undefined.

constraints.description: Number of documents in the input array

examples.input: [{a: 1, b: null}, {c: 2, d: undefined, e: 3}, {f: null, g: 4}]
examples.explanation: The input array has three documents. The first document has a missing field 'b' and a null value. The second document has a missing field 'd' and an undefined value. The third document has a null value. The resulting projected documents are the second and third documents from the input array.

hints.text: Use the $project aggregation operator to specify the fields to include in the projected documents.
Use the $cond aggregation operator to check if a field has a value before including it in the projected documents.

editorial.title: Editorial Solution
editorial.intuition: The pipeline should iterate over the documents and project the fields that have a value. The $project aggregation operator can be used to specify the fields to include in the projected documents. The $cond aggregation operator can be used to check if a field has a value before including it in the projected documents.
editorial.bruteForce: A brute force approach would be to use a loop to iterate over the fields of each document and check if the field has a value. However, this approach would be inefficient and would not take advantage of the MongoDB aggregation pipeline.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution uses the $project aggregation operator to specify the fields to include in the projected documents. The $cond aggregation operator is used to check if a field has a value before including it in the projected documents.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The optimal solution is correct because it uses the $project aggregation operator to specify the fields to include in the projected documents and the $cond aggregation operator to check if a field has a value before including it in the projected documents.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run: [1] Create a MongoDB collection with the input data. [2] Run the aggregation pipeline on the collection. [3] The resulting projected documents are returned as an array.
commonMistakes: Including fields with a value of null or undefined in the projected documents.
Failing to handle documents with missing fields.

functionSignature.python:
class Solution:
  def aggregate_pipeline(self, documents: list);

testCases.input: [{a: 1, b: null}, {c: 2, d: undefined, e: 3}, {f: null, g: 4}]
[{a: 1, b: 2}, {c: null, d: null}, {e: undefined, f: undefined}]
